summary: Sparse mirror with LFS for binaries (.docx, .xlsx, .pdf); R&L is classified reference_only per Phase 4 section 11; agent-only content channel pattern per Phase 4 section 14 governs read access.
notes: Drive remains primary authoring surface; git provides agent reading access without ballooning repo. Migration plan defines which 5-10 high-value R&L assets get LFS-tracked first.

title: Temp ingress timebox: opt-timebox
summary: Hybrid with timeboxed staging. temp-move-or-delete-*/ folders are acceptable provided every file has a target-path within 2 weeks; after timebox expires, the temp folder enters CRITICAL state and operator must triage.
notes: Validator (scripts/validate_temp_ingress.py NEW) reads timestamp from folder name + flags overdue.

summary: Build full ENGAGEMENT_REGISTRY.csv + Supabase mirror + ERP panel slot in Phase 8. Engagement count already 4-5 (SUEZ, Asesoria, Service Management internal, future advisers, future Holistik Researcher cohort); build cost low; discipline payoff high.

summary: Programs canonical at System Owner/programs/<product>/ (governance home); product-specific docs at Envoy Tech Lab/<product>/ (artifact home). Aligns with WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT sub-mark functional split (governance vs delivery).

summary: No AKOS mention in customer-facing prose anywhere; customer packs use 'Holistika OS' or 'the Holistika methodology' only. Codified in BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md section 4.

Both active now (not forward-looking) per operator's Batard precedent + EFA maintenance request.

title: baseline_organisation.csv schema extension (sub_area + status) + Marketing M3 row migration (hard-remove Social Media Manager + Growth Manager)
summary: Schema extension Option C ratified at P8 section 8.14 inline-ratify gate (operator response 2026-05-13). The plan-time intent for sub-area encoding and soft-state tracking was not crystallised into columns until v3.1; this decision crystallises it as the Marketing M3 redesign lands. title: I71 P2 Tier 1 Vale sibling architecture ratification (deterministic-NLP layer alongside regex chassis; C-71-Vale-1 default warning + C-71-Vale-2 default single-Vocab-pair)
summary: Tier 1 Vale sibling folded into I71 P2 from the I71 P1 strategic review (operator-asked: 'do I have to put a never-ending list of words here?' surfacing the regex-cardinality concern). Vale (https://vale.sh) is the open-source NLP + POS-tagging linter the 2026 brand-governance industry uses as the middle layer between regex linters and LLM judges. Mints scripts/generate_vale_styles.py one-time deterministic generator (~500 LOC; reads brand canonicals via akos.brand_voice_register parsers + CANONICAL_PATHS; emits .vale/styles/Holistika/{LLMToneTells,TicFamilies,MBADeckJargon}.yml + .vale/styles/Vocab/{Holistika,Holistika-rejected}.txt). .vale.ini at repo root carries StylesPath = .vale/styles + MinAlertLevel = warning per C-71-Vale-1 default (promotable to error after 30-day operator UAT). Single Vocab pair per C-71-Vale-2 default (simpler; fewer files; deterministic generation; lower operator maintenance cost). Generator supports --check (CI drift detection) + --dry-run modes. release-gate.py row 'BRAND voice Vale sibling (deterministic-NLP layer; ...)' wired adjacent to the existing voice-register row (per D-IH-71-J in-place extension policy); host-conditional SKIP when vale binary absent. 21-case test suite at tests/test_vale_styles_generator.py (deterministic output + Vale-YAML validity + Vocab correctness + --check semantics + graceful skip on absent canonical; PASS). Vale runs ALONGSIDE the regex chassis (not replacing it): regex catches named violations cheaply; Vale catches grammar patterns regex cannot express (e.g., any superlative-adjective in a customer-facing slide H1).
notes: P2 deliverable. C-71-Vale-1 + C-71-Vale-2 verdicts deferred to coordinator inline-ratify post-execution; defaults (warning + single-pair) ship unchanged. title: I72 regression-amendment closure ratification — R-A..R-F shipped on main without re-opening INIT-72 or cutting v3.2.0
summary: I72 R-F closure (2026-05-15): ratifies the post-P10 regression amendment cycle R-A..R-E as a single closure event. The amendment surfaced via the operator's 9-question regression review request, was reframed via Q1b/Q3b/Q9b inline-ratify around the operator's 'disciplines != roles, no horizontal bloat' principle, and shipped as 5 phase-scoped commits (93f82db R-A + 16f08ae R-B + dd86b22 R-C + ccbf87d R-D + a09fbe6 R-E) plus this closure commit. Net delta: baseline_organisation.csv 82 -> 67 rows (-15); Marketing sub-areas 5 -> 4 (Brand+Storytelling -> Brand & Narrative); 4 RevOps forward-charter specialist rows deleted (disciplines absorbed into RevOps Lead); 6 Marketing sub-role rows deleted (disciplines absorbed into Sub-Area Managers); Marketing/Social/ + Marketing/Growth/ shells removed; 5 Cursor rules promoted to alwaysApply: true; 3 missing SOPs backfilled; 4 stale TODO[I72-...] catalog pointers resolved; 4 wrong adapter-registry paths in akos-executable-process-catalog.mdc corrected. INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-72 remains status: closed (this is a regression-amend cycle, NOT a re-open; analogous to a software release shipping patch-band fixes without bumping the major version). No v3.2.0 SemVer cut per operator framing: v3.1 is still being built, and v3.2 will mark a methodological breakthrough, not a registry consolidation pass. CHANGELOG entries land under [Unreleased]/v3.1 banner. UAT bands A/B/C/D/E re-verified PASS at R-F. Ratifies decisions D-IH-72-AI through D-IH-72-AO en bloc (7 amendment decisions: AI path corrections + AJ alwaysApply audit + AK SOP backfill + AL folder cleanup + AM RevOps slim + AN Marketing sub-role collapse + AO Brand+Storytelling merger).
notes: Operator's framing: 'for me this is part of the same i72 this is nothing but a regression a big one'. Closure decision precedence pattern: D-IH-72-CLOSURE was the original P10 closure; D-IH-72-AP is the regression-amend closure layered on top. Both remain active.

title: Mega vs split scope ratification — single I73 covers 8 strands (Learning + Ethics+Learning + People Ops engagement-lifecycle + Compliance/Ethics boundary + Engagement Model Registry + Historical pattern codification + KB human-readability + Methodology IP minting) across 11 phases
summary: Operator brief 2026-05-15 ratified mega-i73 (single initiative covering 8 strands) vs split into I73a/I73b/I73c siblings vs hybrid (I73 People Ops + Engagement Models then spin off IP). Mega chosen for cross-strand coherence: the engagement-as-unit reframe IS the strand-crossing structural insight; splitting forces it to be re-explained in three places. Pause-fatigue mitigation lives in akos-agent-checkpoint-discipline.mdc cadence heuristic (front-load substantive review at P0+P1; soft-pause for P4+ if validators clean; auto-clear after 24h operator silence per akos-inline-ratification.mdc §6.5).
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_explicit_via_askquestion (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15). Close-out P0 (this commit).

title: Hold-gate reframing — charter-satisfies-gate (bootstrapping reality) vs await-real-hire vs hybrid
summary: Candidate scaffold required first-Holistik-Researcher-hired + People-Ops-Lead-onboarded hold-gates. Bootstrapping reality (operator + Madeira AI O5-1 + ad-hoc collaborators; founder own paid employment funds Holistika bootstrap per FOUNDER_TRAJECTORY_INTERNAL §2) means the missing artefact is the engagement-class taxonomy itself, not the hire. Charter-satisfies-gate: designing the engagement models IS the unblock. The seven engagement classes are today’s reality (operator_self funding bootstrap; RCD Legal full-time arrangement; Madeira AI O5-1; ad-hoc collaborators come and go).
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15; recommended default captured per akos-inline-ratification.mdc §6.5 auto-decision fallback). Close-out P0.

title: ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY home — sibling dimension at People Operations vs cross-area canonical vs Operations/RevOps absorption
summary: Place ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv at docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/People Operations/canonicals/dimensions/ as sibling dimension to ENGAGEMENT_REGISTRY.csv (which lives at People/Compliance/canonicals/dimensions/). Owned by People Operations Lead per DAMA Data Owner pattern (akos-executable-process-catalog.mdc Rule 4). Rejected cross-area (compliance/dimensions/) because ownership ambiguous; rejected Operations/RevOps absorption because HR-side classes (apprentice_learner, hourly_consultant) do not fit RevOps. RevOps cross-references via percentage_collaborator + investor_advisor classes (no FK; cross-link per akos-holistika-operations.mdc no-duplication rule).
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_explicit_via_askquestion (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15). Close-out P1 (CSV path locked at P1 commit).

title: Engagement-model taxonomy — 7 classes (hourly_consultant / milestone_consultant / percentage_collaborator / apprentice_learner / investor_advisor / outsourced_helper / operator_self) vs broader (~10) vs narrower (~5)
summary: Operator brief 2026-05-15 named 7 classes mapped 1:1 to documented historical patterns: Mark-II = apprentice_learner; Bâtard 2020 = investor_advisor + percentage_collaborator hybrid; Fiverr/Cameroon = outsourced_helper (€400/mo cap); RCD Legal = hourly_consultant + percentage hybrid; current L'Oréal Europe arrangement = operator_self carrier. Rejected broader (~10 classes splitting percentage into revenue vs equity; outsourced into portal-mediated vs direct; apprentice into cohort vs 1:1) because most splits have N=1 today (over-engineered). Rejected narrower (~5 classes collapsing hourly+milestone or investor+percentage) because retribution structures differ materially.
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_explicit_via_askquestion (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15). Per-class enum rows + retribution_kind + soc_posture + default_access_level mint at P1 (D-IH-73-H..M). Close-out P1.

title: Outsourced helper SOC posture — separate engagement class with extra access-control SOC vs sub-class of hourly with extra clauses
summary: outsourced_helper is its own engagement class with lower trust + scoped access + redacted KB view + work-product-only handoff (no methodology exposure). Default access_level = 1 or 2 per access_levels.md. Rejected sub-class-of-hourly option because P7 KB-view routing must query SOC posture from the registry (queryable canonical row), not parse free-text contract clauses (clause-readability grep fails canonical-CSV gate discipline). The €400/mo cap class is structurally different in retribution + SOC + KB access from cleared collaborators (Fiverr/Cameroon helper pattern per operator brief 2026-05-15).
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15). Close-out P1 (enum row) + P7 (KB-view low-trust route enforcement).

title: Methodology IP brand-vs-name — Holistika brand by default vs operator personal name by default vs decision-deferred-with-criteria-matrix
summary: Decision-deferred-with-criteria-matrix. P8 codifies criteria (is-this-business-IP vs is-this-personal-method-lineage; commercial-leverage vs intellectual-attribution; trademarkability scope; jurisdiction priority). Per-asset filing decision deferred to filing time. Aligns with LOGIC_CHANGE_LOG BT-01 brand-as-shield framing (versioning founder’s own understanding under brand umbrella precedes solid proof). Rejected Holistika-by-default because it forecloses personal-name option for methods with strong personal lineage (e.g. method-anchor #2 multinational-governance from BICG 2015-2016 + Volvo 2018-2020 traces personally per FOUNDER_TRAJECTORY_INTERNAL §4). Rejected operator-name-by-default because it dilutes brand IP; future Holistika collaborators cannot carry method-anchors.
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15). Close-out P8 (criteria matrix codified) then per-asset filing decisions thereafter outside I73 scope.

title: KB human-readability personas — 4 personas mapped 1:1 to engagement classes vs 7 personas vs 3 personas
summary: 4 personas (operator-managed / cleared-collaborator / low-trust-outsourced / apprentice) mapped 1:1 to engagement-class buckets from D-IH-73-D: operator_self -> operator-managed; hourly/milestone/percentage/investor -> cleared-collaborator; outsourced_helper -> low-trust-outsourced; apprentice_learner -> apprentice. Implementation = hlk-erp panel filter routes per akos-mirror-template.mdc (sibling-repo carry-over; not a new app). Each route filters existing KB view by access_level + engagement_model_id. Low-trust route enforces D-IH-73-E SOC posture. Rejected 7-personas-mapped-1:1-to-all-classes because most classes overlap in KB access posture (cleared-collaborator covers hourly + milestone + percentage + investor naturally). Rejected 3-personas (cleared / scoped / restricted) because it collapses operator-managed + apprentice into cleared, losing the apprentice learning-curriculum overlay.
notes: decision_source: operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip (Gate 1 AskQuestion 2026-05-15). Close-out P7.
